summary: Design guide for envelope field auto-injection: shared helper injectEnvelope() in lib/envelope.ts, called BEFORE schema validation in each command, with per-artifact-type configuration registry. artifact_id generation follows deterministic patterns per command. status=drafted enforced in task create. Full backward compatibility via no-clobber semantics.
concerns: Injection must happen BEFORE validation so const fields pass schema checks
enrichTimestamp ownership of created_at must remain untouched
evidence command force-overwrite trust boundary must not be changed
design_guide.placement: Shared helper function injectEnvelope(artifactType, data, context) in new file src/cli/src/lib/envelope.ts. NOT per-command inline. Rationale: 4 commands share identical logic (version, artifact_type, producer_type injection). Duplication across commands creates drift risk. A registry-based approach maps artifact_type to its const values and default producer_type.
design_guide.timing: BEFORE schema validation. The whole point is that agents omit const fields, so the data will fail validation without injection. Flow becomes: readInputData -> injectEnvelope -> validate -> writeJsonFile (which calls enrichTimestamp). This preserves enrichTimestamp sole ownership of created_at/updated_at.
design_guide.registry_shape: Record<string, { version: string; artifact_type: string; producer_type: string; artifact_id_fn: (data, context) => string }>. Keys: task_contract, mission_spec, design_brief, phase_review. Evidence is EXCLUDED from this registry.
design_guide.artifact_id_rules.task_cont: tc-{task_id} where task_id comes from data.task_id
design_guide.artifact_id_rules.mission_s: spec-{mission_id} where mission_id comes from CLI --id flag
design_guide.artifact_id_rules.design_br: brief-{mission_id} where mission_id comes from CLI --id flag
design_guide.artifact_id_rules.phase_rev: phase-{mission_phase}-{timestamp} where mission_phase comes from data.mission_phase
design_guide.status_enforcement: In task create command only, after injectEnvelope and before validate: if data.status is missing or not drafted, force data.status = drafted. This is command-level logic, not envelope logic, because status enforcement is specific to task create semantics.
design_guide.backward_compatibility: No-clobber semantics: injectEnvelope only sets a field if it is undefined or missing in the input data. If an agent explicitly provides version, artifact_type, producer_type, or artifact_id, those values pass through unchanged. Schema validation then catches any incorrect explicit values (e.g., wrong version). This means existing callers that send all fields continue to work identically.
design_guide.evidence_exclusion: The evidence command already force-overwrites version, agent, task_id, role from CLI flags (lines 105-108 of evidence.ts). This is a TRUST BOUNDARY: evidence metadata is derived from CLI flags, not agent input. Envelope injection must NOT touch evidence. The registry simply does not include an evidence entry.
design_guide.implementation_sequence: 1. Create src/cli/src/lib/envelope.ts with ENVELOPE_REGISTRY and injectEnvelope function. 2. Update task.ts create action: add injectEnvelope call before validate, add status=drafted enforcement after. 3. Update mission.ts write-spec action: add injectEnvelope call before validate. 4. Update mission.ts write-brief action: add injectEnvelope call before validate. 5. Update phase.ts write action: add injectEnvelope call before validate. 6. Add unit tests for injectEnvelope (no-clobber, all artifact types, missing fields).
design_guide.function_signature: injectEnvelope(artifactType: string, data: Record<string, unknown>, context: { missionId?: string; taskId?: string }): void. Mutates data in place (consistent with enrichTimestamp pattern). Returns void.
